site.intro: 統計課本教你怎麼算，很少告訴你這些東西當初是為了什麼問題被發明出來，又是在哪裡開始被誤用。這個站一次處理一個看起來理所當然、來歷卻可疑的概念：它為什麼長成這樣，是誰為了什麼發明它，以及它壞掉的時候會壞在哪裡。文章裡的模擬都可以親手轉動，因為有些事情得自己按下去重跑一萬次才會相信。

articles.label: 文章
articles.blurb: 一篇處理一個概念，從它的來歷讀到它的誤用。

glossary.label: 術語表
glossary.blurb: 文章裡用到的每個術語，先一句話講定義，再給一個真實發生過的例子，以及它最常在哪裡被讀反。術語在正文中出現時，同一份定義會浮出來，不必離開閱讀。

site.note: 每個例子都查得到出處，出處就掛在句子旁邊；模擬用固定的亂數種子，同樣的按鈕在任何一台機器上跑出同樣的數字。整站中英雙語，右上角切換。

en.intro: Textbooks teach you how to compute. They rarely tell you what problem a method was invented to solve, or where it started being misused. This site takes one such idea at a time — one that looks self-evident and has a questionable history — and asks why it has the shape it has, who built it and for what, and what exactly breaks when it is misapplied. The simulations are yours to turn, because some things you only believe after re-running them ten thousand times yourself.

articles.label: Articles
articles.blurb: One idea per piece, read from where it came from to where it goes wrong.

glossary.label: Glossary
glossary.blurb: Every term the articles lean on: a one-line definition, an example that actually happened, and the way it is usually misread. Where a term appears in the prose, the same definition surfaces in place, so you never have to leave the page to look it up.

en.note: Every example has a source, and the source hangs beside the sentence. The simulations run on fixed seeds, so the same button gives the same numbers on any machine. The whole site is in both languages; the switch is at the top right.

topics.label: 統計推論
topics.blurb: 假設檢定、估計、信賴區間——被用得最多、也被誤解得最深的一塊。

en.label: Inference
en.blurb: Hypothesis tests, estimates, intervals — the most used and the most misread part of the subject.

articles.title: 怎麼證明「沒有差別」
articles.subtitle: 檢定力與等價檢定：把「沒測到差異」變成「差異小到不重要」
articles.summary: 一個檢定跑出 p = 0.56，你不能因此說兩者相同——不顯著可能只是這個研究沒力氣把差異看清楚。本文用學名藥的生體相等性當例子，說明檢定力（研究看得見多大的效果）與等價檢定（TOST：把效果正面關進一個事先訂好的邊界），以及為什麼「不顯著」要拆成「能排除任何重要差異」與「只是測不到」兩種。

en.title: How to Show There Is No Difference
en.subtitle: Power and equivalence tests: turning "we found no difference" into "any difference is too small to matter"
en.summary: A test returns p = 0.56, and you cannot conclude the two are the same — a non-significant result may just mean the study had no power to see the difference. This article uses drug bioequivalence to explain statistical power (how large an effect a study can see) and equivalence testing (TOST: bounding the effect inside a margin fixed in advance), and why "not significant" has to be split into "we can rule out any meaningful difference" and "we simply could not tell".

articles.title: 為什麼叫虛無假設
articles.subtitle: 從一位喝茶的女士到一份聯署聲明：p 值爭議的觀念史
articles.summary: 「虛無」這個譯名，把 null 讀成了「什麼都沒發生」。Fisher 的 null 講的是可推翻性：實驗設下它，就是為了讓資料有東西可以反駁。這篇追一條線——品茶實驗如何生出第一個 p 值，Neyman 與 Pearson 如何把它改成一套決策規則，課本又如何把兩套邏輯焊成一個誰都沒設計過的東西，以及焊接處會裂在哪裡。

en.title: Why It Is Called the Null Hypothesis
en.subtitle: From a lady tasting tea to a signed statement: a short history of the p value quarrel
en.summary: Null was never about nothingness. Fisher's null is the hypothesis that can be nullified — set up so the data have something to argue against. This piece follows one line: how a tea party produced the first p value, how Neyman and Pearson turned it into a decision rule, how textbooks welded the two together into something neither man designed, and where that weld cracks.

articles.title: 大法官的差異有多大？
articles.subtitle: 用貝氏階層模型處理小樣本比例與部分匯聚
articles.summary: 甲在 4 件參與案件中提出 3 件個別意見書，乙在 100 件中提出 40 件。75% 與 40% 可以描述已觀察的案件，卻不足以支持排名。本文用二項模型、後驗分布與部分匯聚，說明小樣本比例應如何估計與報告。

en.title: How Large Are the Differences Between Justices?
en.subtitle: Small-sample proportions and partial pooling in a Bayesian hierarchical model
en.summary: Justice A files a separate opinion in 3 of 4 participating cases; justice B does so in 40 of 100. The two proportions describe the observed cases, but they do not justify a ranking. This article uses a binomial model, posterior distributions, and partial pooling to show how small-sample proportions can be estimated and reported.

glossaryGroups.label: 檢定的機件
glossaryGroups.blurb: 一次檢定跑起來要用到的零件：假設、分佈、機率、門檻。

en.label: The machinery of a test
en.blurb: The parts a test is assembled from: the hypothesis, the distribution, the probability, the threshold.

glossaryGroups.label: 它會怎麼出錯
glossaryGroups.blurb: 同一套機件在錯的地方會給出錯的答案，這幾個詞講的是錯在哪、錯多少。

en.label: How it goes wrong
en.blurb: The same machinery gives wrong answers in the wrong places. These terms are about which way it fails, and by how much.

glossaryGroups.label: 誤用與它的後果
glossaryGroups.blurb: 研究者的實作習慣如何把一套合理的方法變成一台生產假陽性的機器。

en.label: Misuse and its consequences
en.blurb: How ordinary research habits turn a reasonable method into a machine for manufacturing false positives.

glossary.oneLine: 被設計來承受攻擊的那個假設：設下它，是為了讓資料有機會推翻它。

en.oneLine: The hypothesis set up to be attacked: it exists so the data have something to argue against.

glossary.oneLine: 同一個統計量在反覆抽樣下會長成的樣子。p 值與信賴區間都是從它身上量出來的。

en.oneLine: The shape a statistic takes under repeated sampling. Both p values and confidence intervals are read off it.

glossary.oneLine: 假設虛無為真，得到「像這樣或更極端」的結果的機率。

en.oneLine: The probability of a result at least as extreme as the one you got, if the null were true.

glossary.oneLine: 事前選定的門檻：長期而言，你願意讓多少比例的真虛無被錯判為顯著。

en.oneLine: A threshold fixed in advance: the share of true nulls you are willing to reject in the long run.

glossary.oneLine: 把標籤重新洗牌，看看純屬巧合能做到多好，再看真實資料排在哪裡。

en.oneLine: Shuffle the labels, see how well coincidence can do, then see where the real data land.

glossary.oneLine: 一套會生出區間的規則。95% 是這套規則的長期命中率，不是眼前這一個區間的機率。

en.oneLine: A rule for producing intervals. The 95% is the rule's long-run hit rate, not the probability for the one interval in front of you.

glossary.oneLine: 先說清楚多大的差異才算重要，再用資料把效果關進這個範圍。

en.oneLine: State how large a difference would matter, then use the data to bound the effect inside that range.

glossary.oneLine: 兩種錯法：虛無為真卻拒絕它（誤報），虛無為假卻沒拒絕（漏抓）。

en.term: Type I and Type II error
en.oneLine: Two ways to be wrong: rejecting a true null (a false alarm), or failing to reject a false one (a miss).

glossary.oneLine: 效果真的存在時，這個研究抓得到它的機率。

en.oneLine: The probability that a study will catch an effect that is really there.

glossary.oneLine: 效果有多大，用一把不隨樣本數伸縮的尺量出來。

en.oneLine: How large the effect is, on a scale that does not stretch with the sample.

glossary.oneLine: 一邊看結果一邊調分析：多收幾個樣本、換個共變量、換個結果變數，直到 p 掉到門檻底下。

en.oneLine: Adjusting the analysis while watching the result — a few more subjects, another covariate, another outcome — until p drops below the line.

glossary.oneLine: 檢定做得夠多，光靠雜訊也會冒出「顯著」。不校正，門檻就名存實亡。

en.oneLine: Run enough tests and noise alone will hand you a "significant" one. Without correction the threshold means nothing.

glossary.oneLine: 在所有「顯著」的結果裡，真的有效果的佔幾成。它取決於這個領域有多少假說本來就是對的。

en.oneLine: Of everything that comes out "significant," how much is real. It depends on how many hypotheses in the field were true to begin with.

glossary.oneLine: 把 Fisher 的證據邏輯和 Neyman-Pearson 的決策邏輯焊成一套固定動作，兩邊的代價都不付。

en.oneLine: Fisher's evidential logic and Neyman-Pearson's decision logic welded into a fixed routine that pays neither one's price.

glossary.oneLine: 大量已發表的顯著結果，換一組人重做就做不出來。

en.oneLine: A large share of published significant findings do not come back when someone else runs the study again.
